MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-05-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROBERT V. CHIN, TEL. NO. 1-415-256-8188 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DEAN A. CHRISTOPHERSON OF DAWE & CHRISTOPHERSON LLP, TEL. NO. 1-925-256-6677 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - Defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s ("WFB") request for judicial notice, which is unopposed, is GRANTED. Plaintiff Richard John Riley's request for judicial notice, which is unopposed, is GRANTED. WFB moves for summary judgment on the breach of contract cause of action against it on the grounds that: (1) Riley cannot establish all the elements of his claim, specifically he cannot establish that he performed under the 30-day notice provision of the 2011 Consumer Account Agreement ("CCA"); and (2) the defense of release. (See Christopherson Decl., Ex. 3, ex. 1 at p. 11.) WFB's motion for summary judgment is DENIED AS A MATTER OF LAW. The 30-day notice and release provisions are unconscionable. (See Code Civ. Proc., 1670.5.) The CCA is a contract of adhesion, drafted by WFB and offered to Riley without negotiation. (Pltf's Undisputed Facts in Opp. to Mot. for Summ. J., #7; Riley Decl., 25). The 30-day notice and release of liability provisions are substantively unconscionable in that they are one-sided without reasonable justification, overreaching and unfair. They reallocate the risks of the bargain in an objectively unreasonable and unexpected way. The notice provision effectively eviscerates the statute of limitations without negotiation and without allowing adequate time for the depositor to pursue a remedy. (See 3 Witkin, Cal. Proc. (5th Ed. 2008) Actions, sec. 469, p. 595.) The release provision is invoked to give effect to the notice provision; as such, it is unconscionable for the same reasons. Order signed in open court. Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976

Off Calendar Oct-25-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Sustained without leave to amend as to the second, third, and fourth causes of action. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. The tort claims are barred by the litigation privilege, Cal. Civ. Code 47(b). The Complaint states that it was represented to Wells Fargo in the Notice of Levy and Writ of Execution "that Plaintiff account holder was obliged to the judgment." (First Amended Complaint, 27.) Claims "based on the instructions given to the bank by a Sheriff's Department employee [are] barred by the litigation privilege." (Tom Jones Enterprises, Ltd. v. County of Los Angeles (2013) 212 Cal.App.4th 1283, 1294.) As to the fifth cause of action, the contract attached as Exhibit A to the FAC and extensively quoted in the FAC explicitly states that it is "Effective April 1, 2013." The alleged breach occurred on May 29, 2012, and therefore, the attached and quoted contract could not have been the operative agreement at the time of the alleged breach. Plaintiff has 10 days leave to amend, if he can in good faith, facts relating to the operative contract at the time of the alleged breach. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR #6185 (302/EHG)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-25-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ULRIC USHER AND PATRICIA HAMILTON'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S FIRST, SECOND, THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TO STRIKE THE ENTIRE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AS AGAINST THESE MOVING PARTIES

Appearances: Robert V. Chin of the Law Offices of Robert V. Chin (415.256.8188) for plaintiff and responding party; Ulric E. J. Usher of the Law Offices of E. J. Usher (661.723.0046) for defendants and moving parties.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Denied. The Court finds that the gravamen of Plaintiff's complaint arises out of the wrongful withholding of money by Defendants, and therefore does not arise from a protected activity. (Wallace v. McCubbin (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 1169, 1190, "for anti-SLAPP purposes this gravamen is defined by the acts on which liability is based, not some philosophical thrust or legal essence of the cause of action.") Plaintiff's evidence establishes that Plaintiff notified Defendants multiple times that they levied on the wrong account and Defendants have wrongfully refused to return the funds. (See Complaint, 9; Decl. of Richard John Riley, 12-17.) Thus, Defendants did not make the requisite threshold showing under CCP 425.16 that Plaintiff's causes of action arise out of an act in furtherance of a person's right to petition or free speech, and must be denied. Plaintiff's request for attorney's fees and costs in the amount of $5,500 is granted. Plaintiff has established that this motion was frivolous. The Court takes defendants' Request for Judicial Notice and Evidentiary Objections under submission. Plaintiff submits a proposed form of order for the Court's consideration. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR #6185 (302/EHG)
